questions.explanation: The highest exponent is 5, so the degree is 5.
The leading coefficient is the coefficient of the highest-degree term: −2.
Even degree, positive leading coefficient → both ends go up.
Odd degree, negative leading coefficient → rises left, falls right.

questions.prompt: Which is NOT a polynomial in one variable?
questions.explanation: 1/x = x⁻¹ has a negative exponent, so x + 1/x is NOT a polynomial.
A quartic function is defined as a polynomial of degree 4.

questions.prompt: The zeros of a polynomial are the x-values where:
questions.explanation: Zeros occur where f(x) = 0, i.e. where the graph crosses or touches the x-axis.

questions.prompt: A polynomial of degree 7 has at MOST how many real zeros?
questions.explanation: A polynomial of degree n has at most n real zeros.

questions.prompt: f(x) = 5x⁶ − 2x has degree ____ and leading coefficient ____.
questions.explanation: The highest power is 6 (degree = 6); its coefficient is 5 (leading coefficient = 5).
Odd-degree polynomials always cross the x-axis at least once → at least one real zero.

questions.prompt: The standard form of a polynomial lists terms in:
questions.explanation: Standard form lists terms from greatest to least degree (decreasing).

questions.prompt: If f(x) = x³ + 2x² − 3x = x(x+3)(x−1), the zeros are:
questions.explanation: Set each factor to zero: x = 0, x + 3 = 0 → x = −3, x − 1 = 0 → x = 1.

questions.prompt: Which function is a monomial function?
questions.explanation: A monomial function has only one term: f(x) = 3x⁴.
Odd degree (5), negative leading coefficient (−4) → rises left, falls right.
The highest-degree term is 2x · 4x² = 8x³, so the degree is 3.
